---
title: "anger awareness plan"
format: html
---

## Anger Awareness Plan

Anger can narrow attention and increase impulsive choices. Early warning signs usually appear in the body first (muscle tension, heat, fast breathing, racing heart).

If you catch anger early, you can use short de-escalation actions before it turns into harmful behavior or relapse risk.

Interpersonal stress is a common relapse risk. Communication habits, emotional reactivity, and body language all affect how quickly conflict escalates and how supported you feel afterward.

When communication becomes clearer and more regulated, stress drops and problem-solving improves. These skills protect recovery by reducing isolation, resentment, and impulsive reactions during difficult interactions.

::: callout
### Instructions
Track one anger episode and build a personalized interruption plan.
:::

| Trigger | Early body signs | Thoughts | My first safe action | Outcome |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| | | | | |
| | | | | |
| | | | | |

### My De-Escalation Plan

- Pause phrase:
- Exit strategy:
- Person to contact:
- Recovery action after calming down:
